applyMediaSettingsOnStart: Apply snapshot media profile on adapter start
camera_id_help: Stable key for webhooks (e.g. auffahrt). Leave empty to derive from name.
camera_mediaFolder_help: Folder name under /var/lib/motioneye (e.g. Bambu). Applied on adapter start when MotionEye config sync is enabled. Leave empty to keep the MotionEye default (Camera<ID>). Does not rename existing folders on disk.
camera_name_help: ioBroker channel name — independent of MotionEye label
cameras_info: Add each camera you want in ioBroker. The MotionEye ID is shown in the MotionEye web UI or via /config/list on port 8765. After saving, restart the adapter instance to create datapoints and write webhook URLs to MotionEye.
disableStreamOnStart: Disable video stream on adapter start
help_cameras:
On the Cameras tab use Load cameras from MotionEye to fetch the camera list from MotionEye and add missing rows to the table (existing entries are kept). The adapter instance must be running for this button.

MotionEye ID: In the MotionEye web UI open a camera → Video Device → field Camera ID. Or query /config/list on port 8765.

Display name: ioBroker channel name — can differ from the MotionEye label.
Internal ID: Stable webhook key (e.g. auffahrt). Leave empty to derive from the display name.
Media folder: Optional folder name under /var/lib/motioneye. Applied on adapter start when config sync is enabled.
help_connection:
MotionEye host — IP or hostname of your MotionEye server.
MotionEye config API port — default 8765 (mode, webhooks, stream).
Motion HTTP port — default 7999 (snapshots).
Control MotionEye via config API — leave enabled for normal use. The adapter writes mode, webhook URLs, and stream on/off to MotionEye; ioBroker controls detection and live streams.

Webhook host — IP of your ioBroker host as reachable from MotionEye (e.g. 192.168.130.130). Required for webhook URLs.
Webhook port — default 8090; allow inbound traffic on the ioBroker host.
help_intro:
The MotionEye adapter connects MotionEye cameras to ioBroker: motion detection via webhooks, snapshots, live streams, and mode control (off / still / sharp).

Info states live under motioneye.<instance>.0_info (connection, versions, cameras online).
help_modes:
ModeDescription
offMotion detection off
stillMotion trigger only — no video recording
sharpMotion detection + video recording
help_readme:
Full documentation:

• Adapter README on GitHub
help_setup:
1. Set MotionEye host, credentials, and webhook host on the Settings tab.
2. On the Cameras tab click Load cameras from MotionEye (instance must be running) or add rows manually.
3. Adjust display names and optional media folders.
4. Save the instance configuration and restart the adapter — datapoints are created and webhook URLs are written to MotionEye.
help_states:
StateRWDescription
modeyesyesCamera mode: off, still, sharp
motionyesnoMotion detected (webhook, auto-reset)
statusyesnoLast sync / error text
snapshotnoyesTrigger snapshot (button)
streamyesyesLive video stream on/off
streamPulsenoyesShort stream pulse (button)
streamUrlyesnoHTML embed for inventwo / VIS2
webhookUrlyesnoConfigured webhook URL (read-only)
help_stream:
Set stream to true for a live MJPEG stream. Use streamPulse for a timed preview (see Stream pulse auto-off on Settings).

The streamUrl state contains ready-to-use HTML for inventwo camera widgets or VIS2 — updated when the stream state changes.
load_cameras_help: Fetches /config/list from MotionEye and adds cameras not yet in the table. The adapter instance must be running. Save configuration and restart after adding cameras.
load_cameras_result_added: New cameras were added to the table. Save the configuration and restart the adapter instance.
load_cameras_result_none: No new cameras — all MotionEye cameras are already in the table.
motionEyePassword_help: Plain-text password as used for MotionEye web login. Leave empty if no password is set.
motionHost_help: IP address or hostname of your MotionEye server
motionResetMs_help: How long the .motion state stays true after a webhook (default 15000)
streamAutoOffMs_help: Used by streamPulse only — stream turns off after this duration (0 = never)
useMotionEyeConfig_help: Leave enabled for normal use. The adapter writes camera mode, webhook URLs, and stream on/off to MotionEye (port 8765). ioBroker then controls motion detection and live streams; the MotionEye web UI follows as a side effect. Disable only if you configure MotionEye entirely by hand and ioBroker should only receive webhooks.
webhookBind_help: Network interface for the built-in webhook listener (default 0.0.0.0)
webhookHost_help: IP or hostname of your ioBroker host reachable from MotionEye (e.g. 192.168.130.130). Required — without this value the adapter cannot write webhook URLs to MotionEye.
webhookPort_help: Port for motion webhooks from MotionEye (default 8090). Ensure firewall allows inbound traffic.
